summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the work-energy theorem?

W_net = ΔKE = KE_final - KE_initial.

What is the mathematical form of the work-energy theorem?

The work-energy theorem states that net work equals the change in kinetic energy.

A 3 kg object is moving at 6 m/s and experiences a net force that does -54 J of work. What is its final velocity?

KE_i = ½ × 3 × (6)² = 54 J. W = ΔKE → -54 = KE_f - 54 → KE_f = 0 → v_f = 0 m/s. Wait, let me recalculate: W = KE_f - KE_i → -54 = KE_f - 54 → KE_f = 0, so v_f = 0. But the option is 2 m/s. Let me verify: if v_f = 2, KE_f = ½ × 3 × 4 = 6 J, so W = 6 - 54 = -48 J (not -54). Recalculating with correct answer

A 5 kg object has work of 75 J done on it, starting from rest. What is its final kinetic energy?

W = ΔKE → 75 = KE_f - 0 → KE_f = 75 J.

An object with initial velocity 4 m/s and mass 2 kg has a net force that does 30 J of work. What is its final kinetic energy?

KE_i = ½ × 2 × (4)² = 16 J. KE_f = 16 + 30 = 46 J.

A 3 kg object starting at rest has 108 J of net work done on it. What is its final velocity?

W = ½mv_f² → 108 = ½ × 3 × v_f² → v_f² = 72 → v_f ≈ 8.49 m/s.

An object of mass 4 kg changes from 3 m/s to 7 m/s. What is the net work done on it?

W = ½ × 4 × (7² - 3²) = 2 × (49 - 9) = 2 × 40 = 80 J.

A force does 500 J of work on a 10 kg object initially at rest. If friction does -100 J of work, what is the final kinetic energy?

Net work = 500 - 100 = 400 J. W_net = ΔKE = 400 J.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: If multiple forces act on an object, how do you calculate total work?

The net work is the algebraic sum of all individual works.
